title: aleuritia farinosa subsp. alpigena
intro: Aleuritia farinosa subsp. alpigena, also known as alpine aletris, is a perennial plant that belongs to the lily family (Liliaceae) and is native to the mountainous regions of Europe. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and expectorant actions, making it effective in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The health benefits of alpine aletris include its ability to reduce inflammation, alleviate congestion, and soothe respiratory tract irritations. In herbal preparations, alpine aletris is often dried and used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
